zh.lifeSummary: 當Agnes Dawney於1404年出生在英國英格蘭越可爾艾史可利可時，她的父親Sir John D’Aunay40歲，母親Ellen De Barden34歲。她於1435年與Peter Weston結婚。他們至少是4個兒子的父母。她去世於1460年在英國林可爾史爾享年56歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Agnes Dawney于1404年出生在英国英格兰越可爾艾史可利可时，她的父亲Sir John D’Aunay40岁，母亲Ellen De Barden34岁。她于1435年与Peter Weston结婚。他们至少是4个儿子的父母。她去世于1460年在英国林可爾史爾享年56岁。

nameMeanings.name: Dawney
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Dawnay
meanings: Norman, English: locative name from one of the many places in northern France named from an alder grove (Old French aulnai, aulnoi, latinized as alnetum), such as Annay (Pas-de-Calais), Aulnay (Aube, Marne, Calvados), Aulnois (Aisne), and Aunay (Calvados, Eure-et-Loire, Seine-et-Oise). With fused preposition, Anglo-Norman French d'Aunai became Dawnay, Dawney and (with loss of the final syllable) Dawn. Laney is another form of this surname with fused definite article. See also Dando , from Anglo-Norman French d'Aunou, with which d'Aunai may sometimes have been confused.

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Agnes Dawney was born in 1404, in Escrick, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Sir John D’Aunay, was 40 and her mother, Ellen De Barden, was 34. She married Peter Weston in 1435. They were the parents of at least 4 sons. She died in 1460, in Lincolnshire, England, at the age of 56.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Agnes Dawney est née en 1404, à Escrick, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir John D’Aunay, avait 40 ans et sa mère, Ellen De Barden, avait 34 ans. Elle a épousé Peter Weston en 1435. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils. Elle est décédée en 1460, à Lincolnshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 56 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Agnes Dawney 1404 in Escrick, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir John D’Aunay 40 und ihre Mutter Ellen De Barden 34. Sie hat 1435 Peter Weston geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne. Sie starb 1460 im Alter von 56 Jahren in Lincolnshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Agnes Dawney è nata il nel 1404 nel Escrick, Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Sir John D’Aunay, aveva 40 anni e sua madre, Ellen De Barden, aveva 34 anni. Lei sposato Peter Weston nel 1435. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli. Morì nel 1460, nel Lincolnshire, England, all'età di 56 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Agnes Dawney(이)가 1404년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 스크릭에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir John D’Aunay(은)는 40세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Ellen De Barden(은)는 34세였다. 그녀는 1435년 Peter Weston(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1460년 영국 링코실르에서 56세의 나이로 사망했다.
